title: Improve unique case support
body:
IEEE 1800-2017 12.5.3 requires simulators to issue a violation report
1.  if more than two case items matches (both unique and unique0)
2.  if no case item matches (only unique case)

These checks are not implemented yet.

I think `%Warning-CASEINCOMPLETE` is not necessary for `unique case` even for non-enum types such as logic.
I understand `unique case` is successor of `full_case unique_case` directive for synthesis in the past.
Designer wants uncovered case item to be treated as a dont_care for optimization.

I'd like to find the common interpretation of the spec before making a PR.
